title: Model-routing policy rule + escalation ladder (deploys to all tools)
description: Parent POSTFABLE-EPIC. AC: routing table (interactive/worker/finder/panel/judge tiers); cheap-first ladder w/ gate-failure escalation; consumed by subagents rule, completion-gate, prgroom chains.
notes:
Spec PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/222 — docs/specs/2026-07-04-model-routing-policy-and-escalation-ladder.md (one spec with vaac.3). Archetype-keyed routing table, two-speed bounded ladder, $100/mo default metered-only ceiling, ~/.agents/spend.jsonl ledger. 7 ASSUMPTION flags in §12 for owner scan.
2026-07-05 specfest repair sweep (work-lifecycle spec section 12): claim released - spec merged in PR #220 but implementation never started; bead was invisible to dispatch while in_progress. Now open + brainstormed; implementation-ready retained pending owner scan of the spec assumption ledger. Continuations split across the two objectives sharing the PR #220 spec.
2026-07-05: assumption ledger blessed by owner as-is (specfest verdict fork, work-lifecycle spec section 12). Bead is dispatchable implementation work; no manifest children to mint (spec predates the Continuations requirement; this bead is the implementation unit).

title: Cost telemetry: ccusage/OTel session costs + prgroom usage records + weekly rollup

Scope for THIS bead: §4 ledger bridge (one spend.jsonl record per completed prgroom dispatch, archetype/rung/provider mapping, estimated flag) + §5 spend_rollup.py weekly rollup script (metered-vs-ceiling, per-archetype/model table, heavy-gate-run rows, ccusage session section) + guide docs. notes:
Spec merged to main in PR #227 (2026-07-04). Bead is implementation-ready; spec is the canonical plan.

2026-07-05: assumption ledger blessed by owner as-is (specfest verdict fork, work-lifecycle spec section 12). Bead is dispatchable implementation work; no manifest children to mint (spec predates the Continuations requirement; this bead is the implementation unit).

title: Project-scoped installable content: detection and conditional staging
description: Project-scoped installable content: detection and conditional staging (§8 of docs/specs/2026-07-06-profiles-scope-routing.md, superseding the original 07-04 spec's §7). Under the profiles-only scope-routing redesign, project scope is a binding a run either has or doesn't: --project <path> binds the 'project' scope to that path (explicit opt-in, locked decision preserved) and structurally excludes user-scope writes that run. This bead is rollout slice S2: --project flag, per-tool project_namespaces() support matrix, kits/ source tree (src/kits/<kit>/, e.g. the beads PRIME.md kit), project-local receipt at <project>/.agents-config/install-receipt.json, and the cwd detection-suggests-only line. Depends on agents-config-uxns2.1's S1 (manifest+resolver) landing first — the resolver and scope-binding mechanism this bead materializes into project writes.
acceptance_criteria: Installer can detect at least one project-level condition (TBD by design) and stage project-specific content into the project directory. User is notified or prompted before any project-directory write. Mechanism is extensible to additional detection rules. Scope overlap with Epic F plugin system is resolved with a clear boundary decision.
notes:
Open design questions: (1) Detection contract — .beads/ presence, project-config.toml fields, or both? (2) Trigger model — auto-silent, detect+prompt, or explicit opt-in flag? (3) Scope boundary vs Epic F plugins — is project-scoped content a plugin concern (plugin targets project dir) or a separate mechanism? (4) Collision model — does the Epic E matrix apply to project-scope paths, or does project-level always win?
Owner decision 2026-07-04 (interview, session e5fd807a): trigger model = explicit opt-in command (bead open-Q2 resolved). Detection only suggests — installer may detect a candidate project (.beads/ etc.) and print a suggestion, but never writes to a project directory except via the explicit project-scoped command (e.g. install.sh --project <path> / subcommand; exact surface TBD by spec). Auto-silent and mid-install detect+prompt rejected. Remaining interview scope (project-local boundary, overlay mechanism) deferred to a dedicated brainstorm session with Scott.
Design spec PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/224 (docs/specs/2026-07-04-user-overlay-profiles-project-staging.md)
2026-07-05 repair sweep: claim released - PR #224 only merged the design spec doc (docs/specs/2026-07-04-user-overlay-profiles-project-staging.md); no implementation code landed. Acceptance criteria (detection + staging mechanism) unmet. Reopened as brainstormed + implementation-ready, matching sibling uxns2.1 treatment.
2026-07-06 fablize revision in progress (session 4936162e): owner-directed re-open of 07-04 spec §7 — project staging being redesigned under profiles-only scope routing (locked explicit-opt-in trigger decision is preserved). implementation-ready stripped pending revised-spec merge.
2026-07-08 session paused pending merge: same PR #234 as agents-config-uxns2.1 (docs/specs/2026-07-06-profiles-scope-routing.md), which now also covers this bead's §7 project-staging scope under the profiles-only routing redesign (project scope = an unbound-scope binding structurally, not a special rule). Full status/blocker detail is on uxns2.1's notes — see that bead. Claim released to resume later. TO RESUME: after PR #234 merges, this bead's description needs updating to point at the new spec (was: superseded 07-04 spec §7; now: 2026-07-06-profiles-scope-routing.md §8), then re-stamp implementation-ready alongside uxns2.1.

title: [Impl] Add foreign-agent review pass to ralf-review skill
description:
Spec externalized to docs/beads/abn9.11-add-foreign-agent-review-pass.md (~73KB, exceeds bd notes safe cap of ~65KB). The bead-as-plan convention is preserved by reference: that file IS the canonical plan.

Provenance:
- Brainstorm molecule: agents-config-wisp-cqje (brainstorm-bead formula)
- Crit review: converged round 6 with zero unresolved comments (29 findings resolved across 2 rounds)
- ralf-review: 3-cycle adversarial pass (cap exhausted)
  - Cycle 1: 0B / 2C / 10M / 3m (15 findings, all addressed)
  - Cycle 2: 1B / 2C / 6M / 3m (12 findings, all addressed)
  - Cycle 3: 0B / 1C / 8M / 4m (13 findings, all addressed)
  - Final score: PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS (cap exhausted; final cycle had no blocking/critical structural issues after fixes; remaining concerns are the time-boxed pre-abn9.12 LLM-vs-deterministic execution gaps explicitly documented in R11.2)

Follow-on beads:
- agents-config-abn9.12 (P0, fast-follow, blocks-on this bead) — deterministic orchestration wrapper
- agents-config-3f52 (P1, discovered-from this bead) — spec-modularization skill

Remaining concerns documented inline in the spec doc as Open Questions; all are bounded by the abn9.12 fast-follow.
